source: Future Policy in Malaya - Proposed constitution for the Malayan Union__CO273:675:11
filename: Future Policy in Malaya - Proposed constitution for the Malayan Union__CO273:675:11.txt
summary:
- **Core topic/purpose:** Internal British government planning and approval process for a **new post-war constitution in Malaya**, culminating in a **White Paper** setting out policy for the **Malayan Union** and a separate **Colony of Singapore**.  
- **Geography/time period:** Concerns **Malaya/Malay Peninsula** (Federated & Unfederated Malay States plus **Penang and Malacca**) and **Singapore** (with **Christmas Island** and **Cocos-Keeling Islands**); work spans **Jan 1945–Jan 1946** (with repeated references to post-Japanese occupation transition and end of military administration).  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **Colonial Office** officials (notably **Mr. Gent, Mr. Paskin, Mr. Duncan, Mr. Hastings, W.S. Morgan, A.T. Bourdillon**), the **Malayan Planning Unit (MPU)** (incl. **Col./Brig. Willan, Col. Newboult, Mr. O’Connor**), **Sir Harold MacMichael** (negotiating with Malay Rulers), **Sir George Gater**, **Secretary of State for the Colonies (G.H. Hall)**, **A. Creech Jones**, **Colonial Affairs Committee/Cabinet**, and **Major-General H.R. Hone** (BMA/SEAC).  
- **Major decisions/proposals (constitutional structure):**  
  - Create a **Malayan Union** (Malay States + **Penang & Malacca**) under a **Governor with Executive and Legislative Councils**, plus **local councils** with delegated powers; transitional period where the Governor legislates with an **Advisory Council**.  
  - Make **Singapore a separate Colony** with its own Governor and councils; reconstitute local bodies (e.g., **Municipality**, Harbour Board, Improvement Trust) and broaden representation over time.  
  - Establish **pan-Malayan “common arrangement” subjects** between Union and Singapore (listed examples include **Higher Education, Immigration, Currency, Income Tax, Civil Aviation, Posts & Telegraphs, Shipping**).  
- **Malay Rulers/Sultans—negotiations and role:** Central issue was securing **full jurisdiction for H.M.** in Malay States via agreements negotiated by **Sir Harold MacMichael** (to enable legislation under the **Foreign Jurisdiction Act**). The Rulers’ future role was narrowed: **legislative authority in matters of Mohammedan religion (and discussion of Malay custom earlier in drafting)**, excluding **tithes/taxes**, with **Governor’s assent required** after consultation with a **Central Advisory Council of Sultans**; emphasis on preserving “face,” **civil lists**, and handling of **state property** categories (palaces, mosques, religious lands, etc.) though comprehensive lists were seen as hard to compile.  
- **Citizenship policy (notable numbers/rules):** Proposed **Malayan Union citizenship**: automatic for those **born** in Union/Singapore; also for those **ordinarily resident 10 years out of preceding 15** (with **Japanese occupation disregarded** in calculating the 15 years); possible acquisition after **5 years’ residence**; **oath/affirmation of allegiance** required for those not citizens by birth; non-citizens barred from public office/councils without Governor’s consent.  
- **Notable dates/numbers/events:**  
  - Meetings and revisions throughout **1945** (e.g., discussions **Jan–Mar 1945**, major Sultan-position discussion **28 June 1945**).  
  - White Paper timetable aimed at laying before Parliament around **22 Jan 1946**, alongside the **Straits Settlements (Repeal) Bill**; secrecy stressed pending Cabinet approval; intention for **simultaneous publication in Malaya** via BMA gazettes.  
  - Legislative Council composition issue appears in telegram discussion: initial intent described as **21 official and 21 unofficial members** (with debate over wording “not more than” 21 officials to allow future unofficial majority).
